Q1.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

Q2.v: Forest Department, Government of Chhattisgarh; implementation through Gram Panchayat and community participation channels
Q2.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024 amendment)

Q3.v: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (established April 1, 2018; updated 2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal
Q3.src: programs.md — ANNOUNCEMENT, BUDGET, CAG, OUTCOME, SURVEY, PORTAL entries

Q4.r: The primary resource is the network of 210 registered nurseries (85 government + 125 private, registered under Forest Department authority) through which saplings are produced and then distributed. These nurseries are state-directed assets operating under a government notification, not formally notified for community self-governance or GP jurisdiction. They are state/public resources managed for state-directed environmental objectives (achieving green cover targets, carbon sequestration) rather than for state revenue. Classification A2 applies: state/public resource managed under state authority. The saplings once distributed are planted on private household land, school compounds, and institutional spaces — not on demarcated community commons. T1.1 = A2 qualifies only if T1.2 ≥ 1 (verified below at Q7).
Q4.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q5.v: Vrikshopan Yojana operates through 210 registered nurseries (85 government, 125 private) distributed across Chhattisgarh. Saplings covering 185 species (native, fruit, timber, medicinal) are produced at these nurseries and distributed free of cost to 185,000 households, schools, and institutions. Annual distribution target: 2.5 crore saplings (FY 2025-26); cumulative since 2018: 12.5 crore saplings. Average per household: 135 saplings. Planted area equivalent: approximately 62,500 hectares. No single demarcated commons boundary — saplings are planted at diverse private and public locations chosen by individual recipients. Cost per sapling: ₹12 (100% government-subsidised; zero cost to beneficiary). Survival rate: 65% at 1-year monitoring (above 60% target).
Q5.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q6.r: No demarcated commons resource boundary exists for this scheme. The 210 nurseries are geographically dispersed across the state with no single GP resolution, survey number, GPS boundary, or watershed map identifying a commons resource area. Distributed saplings are planted at individually chosen private and public locations; no public document defines the commons boundary. The scheme tracks environmental outcomes (survival %, CO2 estimates) at aggregate state level, not at a demarcated commons boundary level. FLAG: T4.2 measurement risk triggered — ρ ratio / Vitality Index cannot be computed against a measured boundary.
Q6.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q7.r: The scheme is established by an official government notification (No. 6-VY/2018/CGN) with defined beneficiary categories (households, schools, institutions) and GP-partnered implementation. Access to free saplings is a scheme-defined entitlement available to all eligible citizens — it is not purely executive discretion (access is defined by notification and cannot be arbitrarily withheld outside the scheme framework). However, access does not rise to a statutory or constitutional right in the sense of FRA community forest rights, PESA Gram Sabha mandate, or BMC biodiversity rights. It is an administratively recognised scheme entitlement with a GP implementation role. Score = 1 (customary/administratively recognised right, not purely discretionary).
Q7.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q8.r: No active diversion order, mining lease, change-of-land-use notification, SEZ notification, compulsory acquisition proceeding, or equivalent encumbrance identified on the scheme's operational resources (government nurseries, distribution infrastructure) in source documents reviewed. CAG audit confirms scheme operations unimpeded. PASS.
Q8.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q9.breakdown: Sapling nursery establishment: ₹20 cr; Free sapling distribution: ₹22 cr; Plantation maintenance support: ₹10 cr; Monitoring: ₹3 cr
Q9.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department)

Q10.r: Commons/public-qualifying spend: (a) Sapling nursery establishment ₹20 cr — funds creation and operation of 85 government nurseries, which are public assets. Output (saplings) is produced under public mandate and serves a state-wide distribution function; the nursery infrastructure itself is a public resource asset. (b) Monitoring ₹3 cr — tracks public environmental outcomes (sapling survival, green cover, CO2) at aggregate level; outputs are public information with no private exclusivity. Total commons spend: ₹20 cr + ₹3 cr = ₹23 cr.
Q10.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

Q11.r: Private-benefit spend: (a) Free sapling distribution ₹22 cr — logistics and distribution cost whose direct output flows to individual registered households for planting on private land; the direct beneficiary of the sapling (fruit, timber, medicinal produce) is the private household on private land. (b) Plantation maintenance support ₹10 cr — provides maintenance advice and follow-up support for trees planted on individual private household land; outputs flow exclusively to private assets. Total private spend: ₹22 cr + ₹10 cr = ₹32 cr. Verification: Q10 + Q11 = ₹23 cr + ₹32 cr = ₹55 cr = Q9. Consistent.
Q11.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

Q12.interp: FAIL — θ₁ = 0.418 < 0.50 threshold. Majority of scheme expenditure (₹32 cr out of ₹55 cr, 58.2%) funds direct delivery of saplings to private household plots and private maintenance support. Commons-qualifying spend (government nursery infrastructure + monitoring) = ₹23 cr (41.8%). The scheme's fiscal architecture is primarily a private-good subsidy — providing free saplings for private land planting — rather than an investment in a shared commons resource.
Q12.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

docs: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department) — primary budget document with component breakdown
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — confirms ₹55 cr allocated, ₹52 cr spent (95%); verifies distribution records
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — confirms ₹55 cr budget disbursed and output metrics

Q14.r: Benefits are partially excludable. The scheme operates a beneficiary registration system: 185,000 households are registered in the scheme's enrollment database (confirmed by CAG audit). Non-registered individuals cannot access free saplings through the scheme's distribution calendar. The direct benefits (fruit, timber, medicinal produce from trees planted on private land) accrue exclusively to the registered planting household. While planted trees produce environmental externalities (CO2 sequestration, local biodiversity, microclimate) that are non-excludable, these are secondary outcomes not the primary scheme deliverable. Score = 1: partially excludable — defined registered group with practical exclusion of non-registrants. Note: exclusion is registration-based, not fee-based; no equity cross-flag on T2.2 required.
Q14.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (enrollment database confirmed); Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Vrikshopan Yojana Portal (participant registry — 185,000 households)

Q15.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 ('Participants: 185,000 households'); Outcome Budget 2025-26 ('Participants: 185,000 households'); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 ('Participants: 185,000 registered confirmed (enrollment database)')

Q16.src: The 185,000 registered household beneficiaries are Chhattisgarh residents. The primary resource (210 government and private registered nurseries) is distributed across the state. Under the 'local' definition applied at Q17, all 185,000 registered household beneficiaries qualify as local dwellers — each is within GP/block proximity of at least one of the 210 distributed nurseries. No non-local or out-of-state beneficiaries identified in source documents.

Q17.v: Settled_communities — within GP/block of any of the 210 distributed nurseries across Chhattisgarh (effectively state-wide coverage)
Q17.r: The scheme's primary resource (government and registered private nurseries) comprises 210 facilities distributed across the state of Chhattisgarh rather than a single geographic commons. All 185,000 registered beneficiary households are Chhattisgarh residents and thus within the GP/block proximity of at least one nursery location. The settled communities definition is applied; the geographic unit is Chhattisgarh state (beneficiaries are state residents accessing a state-distributed resource network).
Q17.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q18.interp: PASS — θ₂ = 1.00 ≥ 0.70 threshold. All 185,000 registered household beneficiaries are local Chhattisgarh residents with access to the distributed nursery network. The scheme does not channel benefits to distant or non-local actors.
Q18.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q19.v: <<UNVERIFIED: No scheme-level disaggregation of beneficiaries by SC/ST/de-notified tribe/landless/women-headed/differently-abled-headed/elderly-headed household categories found in any source document. The scheme has no explicit marginalised-household targeting mechanism — it distributes saplings to all registered households, schools, and institutions without a beneficiary socio-economic screening criterion. CAG audit confirms enrollment database of 185,000 households but does not report social category breakdown. Economic Survey, Outcome Budget, and scheme notification are silent on marginalised beneficiary share.>>
Q19.r: Scheme source documents do not contain beneficiary disaggregation by social category. Chhattisgarh's SC+ST population share (~44.8% per Census 2011) could serve as a demographic proxy, but applying a state demographic ratio to scheme beneficiaries without scheme-level data violates the verification requirement. Field survey or scheme MIS extraction with social category fields would be required to verify this parameter.
Q19.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q20.v: <<UNVERIFIED: θ₃ cannot be computed — Q19 (numerator) is unverified>>
Q20.calc: θ₃ = Q19.v ÷ Q15.v — Q19 is UNVERIFIED; calculation cannot be performed
Q20.interp: FAIL — θ₃ cannot be verified against the 0.40 threshold. Unverified field Q19 prevents computation. Absent verified marginalised beneficiary data, the condition cannot be confirmed as met.

22a.r: Gram Panchayat is a formally constituted body under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment and the Chhattisgarh Panchayat Raj Act. It is explicitly named as an implementation body in Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024): 'Implementation: Forest Department + Gram Panchayat + Community participation.'

22b.r: The GP's role stated in the scheme notification is 'implementation' — logistics, distribution coordination, and community participation facilitation. No scheme guideline text mandates a GP decision-making role in scheme governance (e.g., beneficiary approval authority, target-setting, resource allocation decisions, oversight with binding resolutions). The GP functions as an administrative delivery channel, not as a scheme governance body with a decision-making mandate. An implementation role is distinct from a mandated governance/decision-making role per T2.3 requirements.

22c.r: No documentation of scheme-specific GP or community body meetings (≥ 2 meetings with recorded resolutions in prior 12 months) found in source documents. CAG audit, Outcome Budget, and Portal do not reference scheme-specific governance meeting records. The GP may hold regular Gram Sabha meetings for other functions, but no evidence of scheme-specific governance meetings with resolutions for Vrikshopan Yojana is available.

Q22.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q23.v: <<UNVERIFIED: No formally constituted scheme-specific governance body established (T2.3 = 0); women's representation in scheme governance cannot be assessed. Chhattisgarh Panchayat Raj Act mandates 50% women's reservation in GP (exceeds 33% threshold), but the GP has not been established as a scheme governance body with a decision-making mandate.>>
Q23.r: Since T2.3 = 0 (no functional governance body with a mandated scheme decision-making role exists), women's representation in a non-existent scheme governance body cannot be scored. If GP is recognised as the implementation body, CG's 50% women's reservation in GP would satisfy the ≥33% requirement — but this cannot be confirmed as scheme-specific governance representation without T2.3 being satisfied.
Q23.src: Chhattisgarh Panchayat Raj Act (50% women's reservation provision); Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q24.r: No formal grievance or voice mechanism for non-beneficiaries is identified in scheme source documents. The scheme portal lists distribution and species information services but no grievance submission mechanism for excluded citizens. No Social Audit provision, MIS grievance portal, ombudsman, or Gram Sabha complaint process is mandated at scheme level for Vrikshopan Yojana. Since the scheme is open to all registered households (not an exclusive allocation to a limited group), the population of 'non-beneficiaries' is limited to non-registrants — but no mechanism exists for them to contest exclusion. FLAG: governance gap recorded.
Q24.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Vrikshopan Yojana Portal; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q25.r: The scheme's Economic Survey entry and Outcome Budget explicitly record employment generation as a scheme output: 'Nursery workers (12,500) + distribution support (8,500)' = 21,000 total workers. The Official Notification (2024 amendment) identifies implementation through Forest Department + Gram Panchayat + community participation, with nursery operations and distribution logistics creating wage employment. The scheme thus mandates an explicit livelihood pathway (wage employment in nursery production and sapling distribution) articulated in official scheme documents, satisfying condition (b) of T3.1 — an explicit livelihood pathway articulated in scheme design, even if expressed in terms of worker headcount rather than a formal log-frame income indicator.
Q25.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Outcome Budget 2025-26

observed.calc: No independent evaluation study, MIS income tracking, or household income survey for beneficiary households or nursery workers found in source documents. CAG audit verifies distribution and survival metrics only — no income per household data recorded.
observed.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

projected.calc: Scheme guidelines (Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN) and DPR-equivalent budget document do not contain a projected income per beneficiary household per year (ΔY). The Outcome Budget tracks environmental outputs (survival rate, hectares, CO2 potential) and scheme throughput (saplings distributed, households reached) but provides no income projection for the 185,000 beneficiary households. Employment headcount (21,000 workers) is recorded but wage rate and annual income per worker are not specified in any source document.
projected.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q26.r: Neither Observed nor Projected ΔY is available in scheme source documents. All source documents track environmental outputs (survival, green cover, CO2) and scheme throughput (saplings, households, species). No income-per-household data — observed or projected — is recorded for the 185,000 beneficiary families or the 21,000 employed workers. Q26 is UNVERIFIED and cannot be assessed against either threshold (₹3,000 observed or ₹5,000 projected).
docs: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — employment headcount only, no ΔY
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — environmental and throughput metrics only
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — distribution and survival verification only

Q27.r: One distinct livelihood pathway explicitly articulated in scheme documents: (1) Wage employment in nursery production and sapling distribution operations — Economic Survey records 12,500 nursery workers and 8,500 distribution support workers = 21,000 total; Outcome Budget confirms 'Workers: 21,000 (nursery + distribution).' This is a single pathway category (wage employment in scheme operations). Potential long-term income from fruit/timber/medicinal trees planted by the 185,000 beneficiary households is not articulated as a current income pathway in scheme guidelines or DPR — the scheme documents do not project or mandate a produce-income mechanism for planted trees. Score = 1.
Q27.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q28.r: No market or institutional offtake mechanism exists or is mandated for any produce or services generated through this scheme. The scheme distributes saplings for environmental objectives; it does not establish: MSP procurement for produce from planted trees, government agency offtake agreement (TDCCOL/OMC/NAFED) for fruit/timber/medicinal produce, MGNREGS wage payment linkage for nursery workers, GI tag or certification with documented buyer network, BMC access and benefit-sharing agreement, community seed distribution network, fair trade or organic certification with institutional buyer, or state procurement for community produce. Marketing of produce from privately planted trees is left entirely to individual households on open market with no institutional backstop. AUTOMATIC T3 FAIL on this gate.
Q28.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q29.r: Scheme MIS and evaluation framework track environmental metrics only: sapling survival rate (65% at 1-year), green cover (62,500 ha equivalent), CO2 sequestration potential (75 lakh tons, 30-year), species diversity (185 varieties), and nursery quality (92% compliance). No non-monetary livelihood indicators are tracked: food security months, nutritional diversity (food species accessed from trees), livestock feeding days, or access to medicinal plants for beneficiary households. Since Q26 ΔY is UNVERIFIED (cannot establish proximity to threshold), the near-threshold flag for potential under-measurement is noted as a general concern: the scheme's fruit, timber, and medicinal tree distribution likely produces non-monetary livelihood value that is entirely unmeasured.
Q29.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q30.r: No legally binding or scheme-guideline-mandated replenishment/regeneration rule with a defined non-compliance consequence is found for the commons resource (government nurseries, classified A2). The scheme's Official Notification (2024) specifies: (a) '60%+ survival target at 1-year' — this is a performance metric for privately planted trees, not a replenishment obligation for the commons (nursery) resource; (b) '3-year maintenance support' — advisory/extension support for private plantations, not a commons replenishment mandate; (c) nursery restocking is implicit in the annual budget cycle (₹20 cr nursery establishment per FY), but this is operational continuity, not a scheme-guideline-mandated replenishment rule with a stated consequence (fund withholding, disqualification, reduced allocation) for non-compliance. No scheme clause mandating nursery restocking targets with enforcement consequences was identified. RESULT: T4.1 = No → AUTOMATIC T4 FAIL (Near-SOC Structural). The scheme generates sapling distribution from public nursery resources but contains no structural lock against nursery depletion or afforestation shortfall.
Q30.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q31.r: Carried T1.1 = A2 (state-managed government nurseries — a physical/natural commons category). T1.1 ≠ E, therefore the ρ Ratio track applies. Q32 is answered; Q33 is set to SKIP.

Q32.calc: ρ = Annual extraction from commons ÷ Annual replenishment mandated/created by scheme. Proxy method (T1.5 = No; resource un-demarcated; Estimated): Extraction = saplings distributed from nursery stock per year = 2.5 crore (FY 2025-26 target, confirmed by CAG and Outcome Budget). Replenishment = new sapling production capacity per year = 2.5 crore (annual nursery production target per scheme design, ₹20 cr nursery establishment budget supporting 85 government nurseries producing 2.5 cr saplings). ρ = 2.5 cr ÷ 2.5 cr = 1.00.
Q32.interp: PASS on ratio — ρ = 1.00 ≤ 1.2 threshold. However, this result is Estimated (T1.5 = No; no demarcated resource boundary; proxy method used: annual distribution = extraction, annual production target = replenishment). NOTE: Although ρ ≤ 1.2 (ratio PASS), T4a overall FAIL is driven by Q30 (T4.1 = No — no mandatory replenishment rule with enforcement). The ρ calculation also reflects a design feature: the scheme produces saplings for distribution, so extraction and production are equal by design — this does not represent a genuine sustainability lock. Uncertainty: T1.5 = No means this ratio cannot be verified against measured boundary data; re-verify when nursery demarcation data and audited production vs distribution records are available.
Q32.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6
docs: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — annual distribution: 2.5 crore saplings confirmed
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — distribution records verified: 2.5 cr (FY 2024-25)
Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6 — nursery establishment budget ₹20 cr for 2.5 cr production

Q34.r: Annual monitoring is conducted by the Forest Department and verified by CAG. The scheme tracks: sapling survival rate (65% at 1-year, verified by CAG monitoring plot surveys), total saplings distributed (annual government reports), nursery production (210 nurseries audited), quality compliance (92% verified by sample testing). The Outcome Budget and CAG State Accounts Report produce annual performance data. The scheme portal (vrikshopan portal) provides aggregate statistics updated annually. However, monitoring is conducted by government agencies only (Forest Department + CAG audit); it is government-reported at annual intervals, not community-accessible at quarterly frequency or better. Score = 1. Q35 = No → threshold = ≥ 1. PASS on T4.3.
Q34.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal

Q35.r: The scheme's primary commons resource (A2: government nurseries, 210 facilities state-wide) is not classified under a specific notified climate/ecological stress designation in the applicable official databases. The nursery infrastructure as a state-managed asset network does not appear on CGWB over-exploited block lists (groundwater), ISFR high-fire-risk or high-deforestation-pressure designations for the nurseries per se, or NDMA drought-prone block classification for the nursery resource specifically. While Chhattisgarh has some drought-prone districts and faces forest cover pressures (per ISFR), the scheme operates state-wide through 210 distributed nurseries and no specific stress-zone classification is documented for the scheme's commons resource in official sources. Q34 threshold remains at ≥ 1 (standard; no upgrade applied).
Q35.src: CGWB groundwater status reports; ISFR 2023; NDMA drought-prone district list; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q36.r: No penalty or sanction mechanism for violation of replenishment or use rules is identified in any scheme source document. The Official Notification (2024) specifies distribution procedures and survival targets but contains no enforcement clause, fine, scheme-benefit withdrawal, suspension of nursery access, or legal sanction for non-compliance with any scheme obligation. The 60% survival target is a performance indicator with no stated consequence for failure to achieve it. There is no GP-imposed sanction or administrative penalty mechanism. Note: this finding is compounded by Q30 (T4.1 = No) — since no replenishment rule exists, there is correspondingly no penalty framework for its violation.
Q36.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

Q37.r: No post-scheme continuity mechanism is designed into the scheme. The scheme provides a 3-year maintenance support period for planted saplings, but this support period is within the scheme's active phase, not a post-scheme institutional handover arrangement. Once the scheme's support period ends, trees on private land revert to private management with no commons governance continuity. For the nursery infrastructure (A2 commons resource), no O&M fund, GP/BMC institutional vesting, or continuity clause for nursery operations after scheme closure is stipulated in scheme guidelines. The scheme assumes ongoing budget allocation from the state government, but no ring-fenced continuity fund or institutional vehicle beyond the scheme's project period is established. FAIL on T4.5.
Q37.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q38.r: Monitoring is conducted exclusively by government agencies (Forest Department annual monitoring, CAG periodic audit). No scheme guideline mandates community participation in monitoring: no social audit provision, no GP-led participatory resource assessment, no community monitoring committee with defined role, no Village Ecological Register update requirement, no beneficiary-led survival reporting beyond the portal's self-reporting feature (which is informational, not constitutionally or scheme-mandated). The portal's survival tracker allows individual beneficiaries to report tree survival but is not a structured community monitoring mechanism with institutional mandate or formal record-keeping role. FLAG: T4.6 = 0 is the primary structural gap and upgrade lever — community monitoring is the most cost-effective addition to convert Near-SOC (Structural) schemes. Activation plan recommended: mandate GP-level participatory sapling survival audits with recorded resolutions, feeding into the state MIS.
Q38.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

schemes.objectiveText: Vrikshopan Yojana operates through 210 registered nurseries (85 government, 125 private) distributed across Chhattisgarh. Saplings covering 185 species (native, fruit, timber, medicinal) are produced at these nurseries and distributed free of cost to 185,000 households, schools, and institutions. Annual distribution target: 2.5 crore saplings (FY 2025-26); cumulative since 2018: 12.5 crore saplings. Average per household: 135 saplings. Planted area equivalent: approximately 62,500 hectares. No single demarcated commons boundary — saplings are planted at diverse private and public locations chosen by individual recipients. Cost per sapling: ₹12 (100% government-subsidised; zero cost to beneficiary). Survival rate: 65% at 1-year monitoring (above 60% target).

raw.programsMd:
## Vrikshopan Yojana (Sapling Distribution)
- BUDGET: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department) allocates ₹55 crores for Vrikshopan Yojana (sapling distribution + plantation support program). Budget breakdown: Sapling nursery establishment (₹20 cr), Free sapling distribution (₹22 cr), Plantation maintenance support (₹10 cr), Monitoring (₹3 cr). Scope: 2.5 crore saplings distribution (FY 2025-26 target), cumulative 12.5 crore saplings distributed since 2018.
- SURVEY: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25: 'Vrikshopan Yojana promotes citizen participation in tree planting: Sapling distribution (FY 2024-25): 2.5 crore (250 million) distributed free. Cumulative since 2018: 12.5 crore saplings. Participants: 185,000 households (avg 135 saplings per household). Tree species: 185 varieties (native, fruit, timber, medicinal). Sapling survival: 65% at 1-year monitoring (target 60%). Green cover impact: 62,500 hectares equivalent plantations (at 2,000 trees/hectare). Carbon potential: 75 lakh tons CO2 sequestration (30-year maturity). Nurseries: 85 government + 125 private registered. Employment: Nursery workers (12,500) + distribution support (8,500). Cost per sapling: ₹12 (government absorbed cost, zero to beneficiary). Citizen participation: 185,000 families engaged in environmental stewardship.'
- OUTCOME: Outcome Budget 2025-26 results: Saplings distributed (FY 2024-25): 2.5 crore (250 million). Cumulative: 12.5 crore since 2018. Participants: 185,000 households. Per household: 135 saplings average. Tree species: 185 varieties (diversity). Sapling survival: 65% at 1-year (above 60% target). Green cover: 62,500 hectares equivalent. CO2 potential: 75 lakh tons (30-year). Nurseries: 210 total (85 govt + 125 private). Workers: 21,000 (nursery + distribution). Cost per sapling: ₹12 (subsidized 100%). Beneficiary cost: Zero rupees. Quality: 92% saplings meeting standards. Maintenance support: 3-year follow-up available. Budget disbursed: ₹55 cr. Citizen engagement: 185,000 families (environmental participation).
- ANNOUNCEMENT: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN establishes Vrikshopan Yojana April 1, 2018 (mass sapling distribution initiative). Updated notification (2024): Specifies (1) Annual target: 2.5 crore saplings/year. (2) Distribution: Free to citizens (zero cost). (3) Species: 185 approved varieties (native, fruit, timber, medicinal). (4) Nurseries: 210 registered (85 govt + 125 private). (5) Survival: 60%+ target at 1-year. (6) Support: Maintenance advice, water protocol, pest management (3 years). (7) Beneficiaries: Households, schools, institutions. (8) Cumulative: 12.5 crore target by 2026. Implementation: Forest Department + Gram Panchayat + Community participation.
- CAG: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 audits Vrikshopan Yojana. Audit findings: Saplings distributed: 2.5 cr (FY 2024-25) verified (distribution records). Cumulative: 12.5 cr verified (2018-2025 annual reports). Participants: 185,000 registered confirmed (enrollment database). Per household: 135 saplings calculated (total/count). Species: 185 varieties confirmed (approved list, nursery records). Survival: 65% verified (1-year monitoring plot surveys). Green cover: 62,500 hectares estimated (sapling calculation). CO2: 75 lakh tons projected (biomass model). Nurseries: 210 counted (registration audit). Workers: 21,000 estimated (nursery payroll + distribution). Cost per sapling: ₹12 verified (nursery production cost). Beneficiary cost: Zero confirmed (subsidy 100%). Quality: 92% compliance verified (sample testing). Budget: ₹55 cr allocated, ₹52 cr spent (95%). Maintenance support: 3-year protocol verified (extension records).
- PORTAL: Vrikshopan Yojana featured on state forest portal. Portal features: Sapling booking (2.5 cr distribution calendar), species selector (185 varieties), nursery locator (210 registered), participant registry (185,000 households), survival tracker (65% verification), maintenance guide (watering, pest prevention), benefit calculator (CO2 sequestration), distribution schedule. Statistics: 2.5 cr saplings FY 2024-25, 12.5 cr cumulative, 185,000 participants, 135 saplings/household, 185 species, 65% survival, 62,500 hectares, 75 lakh tons CO2, 210 nurseries, ₹55 cr budget. Portal services: Sapling booking, species info, nursery finder, maintenance guide, benefit calculator.

t1.data_source_summary: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (est. 2018, amended 2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal

Q1.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

Q2.v: Forest Department, Government of Chhattisgarh; implementation through Gram Panchayat and community participation channels
Q2.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024 amendment)

Q3.v: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (established April 1, 2018; updated 2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal
Q3.src: programs.md — ANNOUNCEMENT, BUDGET, CAG, OUTCOME, SURVEY, PORTAL entries

Q4.r: The primary resource is the network of 210 registered nurseries (85 government + 125 private, registered under Forest Department authority) through which saplings are produced and then distributed. These nurseries are state-directed assets operating under a government notification, not formally notified for community self-governance or GP jurisdiction. They are state/public resources managed for state-directed environmental objectives (achieving green cover targets, carbon sequestration) rather than for state revenue. Classification A2 applies: state/public resource managed under state authority. The saplings once distributed are planted on private household land, school compounds, and institutional spaces — not on demarcated community commons. T1.1 = A2 qualifies only if T1.2 ≥ 1 (verified below at Q7).
Q4.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q5.v: Vrikshopan Yojana operates through 210 registered nurseries (85 government, 125 private) distributed across Chhattisgarh. Saplings covering 185 species (native, fruit, timber, medicinal) are produced at these nurseries and distributed free of cost to 185,000 households, schools, and institutions. Annual distribution target: 2.5 crore saplings (FY 2025-26); cumulative since 2018: 12.5 crore saplings. Average per household: 135 saplings. Planted area equivalent: approximately 62,500 hectares. No single demarcated commons boundary — saplings are planted at diverse private and public locations chosen by individual recipients. Cost per sapling: ₹12 (100% government-subsidised; zero cost to beneficiary). Survival rate: 65% at 1-year monitoring (above 60% target).
Q5.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q6.r: No demarcated commons resource boundary exists for this scheme. The 210 nurseries are geographically dispersed across the state with no single GP resolution, survey number, GPS boundary, or watershed map identifying a commons resource area. Distributed saplings are planted at individually chosen private and public locations; no public document defines the commons boundary. The scheme tracks environmental outcomes (survival %, CO2 estimates) at aggregate state level, not at a demarcated commons boundary level. FLAG: T4.2 measurement risk triggered — ρ ratio / Vitality Index cannot be computed against a measured boundary.
Q6.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q7.r: The scheme is established by an official government notification (No. 6-VY/2018/CGN) with defined beneficiary categories (households, schools, institutions) and GP-partnered implementation. Access to free saplings is a scheme-defined entitlement available to all eligible citizens — it is not purely executive discretion (access is defined by notification and cannot be arbitrarily withheld outside the scheme framework). However, access does not rise to a statutory or constitutional right in the sense of FRA community forest rights, PESA Gram Sabha mandate, or BMC biodiversity rights. It is an administratively recognised scheme entitlement with a GP implementation role. Score = 1 (customary/administratively recognised right, not purely discretionary).
Q7.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q8.r: No active diversion order, mining lease, change-of-land-use notification, SEZ notification, compulsory acquisition proceeding, or equivalent encumbrance identified on the scheme's operational resources (government nurseries, distribution infrastructure) in source documents reviewed. CAG audit confirms scheme operations unimpeded. PASS.
Q8.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q9.breakdown: Sapling nursery establishment: ₹20 cr; Free sapling distribution: ₹22 cr; Plantation maintenance support: ₹10 cr; Monitoring: ₹3 cr
Q9.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department)

Q10.r: Commons/public-qualifying spend: (a) Sapling nursery establishment ₹20 cr — funds creation and operation of 85 government nurseries, which are public assets. Output (saplings) is produced under public mandate and serves a state-wide distribution function; the nursery infrastructure itself is a public resource asset. (b) Monitoring ₹3 cr — tracks public environmental outcomes (sapling survival, green cover, CO2) at aggregate level; outputs are public information with no private exclusivity. Total commons spend: ₹20 cr + ₹3 cr = ₹23 cr.
Q10.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

Q11.r: Private-benefit spend: (a) Free sapling distribution ₹22 cr — logistics and distribution cost whose direct output flows to individual registered households for planting on private land; the direct beneficiary of the sapling (fruit, timber, medicinal produce) is the private household on private land. (b) Plantation maintenance support ₹10 cr — provides maintenance advice and follow-up support for trees planted on individual private household land; outputs flow exclusively to private assets. Total private spend: ₹22 cr + ₹10 cr = ₹32 cr. Verification: Q10 + Q11 = ₹23 cr + ₹32 cr = ₹55 cr = Q9. Consistent.
Q11.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

Q12.interp: FAIL — θ₁ = 0.418 < 0.50 threshold. Majority of scheme expenditure (₹32 cr out of ₹55 cr, 58.2%) funds direct delivery of saplings to private household plots and private maintenance support. Commons-qualifying spend (government nursery infrastructure + monitoring) = ₹23 cr (41.8%). The scheme's fiscal architecture is primarily a private-good subsidy — providing free saplings for private land planting — rather than an investment in a shared commons resource.
Q12.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

Outcome Budget 2025-26 — confirms ₹55 cr budget disbursed and output metrics

Q14.r: Benefits are partially excludable. The scheme operates a beneficiary registration system: 185,000 households are registered in the scheme's enrollment database (confirmed by CAG audit). Non-registered individuals cannot access free saplings through the scheme's distribution calendar. The direct benefits (fruit, timber, medicinal produce from trees planted on private land) accrue exclusively to the registered planting household. While planted trees produce environmental externalities (CO2 sequestration, local biodiversity, microclimate) that are non-excludable, these are secondary outcomes not the primary scheme deliverable. Score = 1: partially excludable — defined registered group with practical exclusion of non-registrants. Note: exclusion is registration-based, not fee-based; no equity cross-flag on T2.2 required.
Q14.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (enrollment database confirmed); Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Vrikshopan Yojana Portal (participant registry — 185,000 households)

Q16.src: The 185,000 registered household beneficiaries are Chhattisgarh residents. The primary resource (210 government and private registered nurseries) is distributed across the state. Under the 'local' definition applied at Q17, all 185,000 registered household beneficiaries qualify as local dwellers — each is within GP/block proximity of at least one of the 210 distributed nurseries. No non-local or out-of-state beneficiaries identified in source documents.

All 185,000 registered beneficiary households are Chhattisgarh residents and thus within the GP/block proximity of at least one nursery location. The settled communities definition is applied; the geographic unit is Chhattisgarh state (beneficiaries are state residents accessing a state-distributed resource network).

Economic Survey, Outcome Budget, and scheme notification are silent on marginalised beneficiary share.>>

Chhattisgarh's SC+ST population share (~44.8% per Census 2011) could serve as a demographic proxy, but applying a state demographic ratio to scheme beneficiaries without scheme-level data violates the verification requirement. 22a.r: Gram Panchayat is a formally constituted body under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment and the Chhattisgarh Panchayat Raj Act. It is explicitly named as an implementation body in Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024): 'Implementation: Forest Department + Gram Panchayat + Community participation.'

22b.r: The GP's role stated in the scheme notification is 'implementation' — logistics, distribution coordination, and community participation facilitation. No scheme guideline text mandates a GP decision-making role in scheme governance (e.g., beneficiary approval authority, target-setting, resource allocation decisions, oversight with binding resolutions). The GP functions as an administrative delivery channel, not as a scheme governance body with a decision-making mandate. An implementation role is distinct from a mandated governance/decision-making role per T2.3 requirements.

22c.r: No documentation of scheme-specific GP or community body meetings (≥ 2 meetings with recorded resolutions in prior 12 months) found in source documents. CAG audit, Outcome Budget, and Portal do not reference scheme-specific governance meeting records. The GP may hold regular Gram Sabha meetings for other functions, but no evidence of scheme-specific governance meetings with resolutions for Vrikshopan Yojana is available.

Q22.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q23.v: <<UNVERIFIED: No formally constituted scheme-specific governance body established (T2.3 = 0); women's representation in scheme governance cannot be assessed. Chhattisgarh Panchayat Raj Act mandates 50% women's reservation in GP (exceeds 33% threshold), but the GP has not been established as a scheme governance body with a decision-making mandate.>>
Q23.r: Since T2.3 = 0 (no functional governance body with a mandated scheme decision-making role exists), women's representation in a non-existent scheme governance body cannot be scored. If GP is recognised as the implementation body, CG's 50% women's reservation in GP would satisfy the ≥33% requirement — but this cannot be confirmed as scheme-specific governance representation without T2.3 being satisfied.
Q23.src: Chhattisgarh Panchayat Raj Act (50% women's reservation provision); Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q24.r: No formal grievance or voice mechanism for non-beneficiaries is identified in scheme source documents. The scheme portal lists distribution and species information services but no grievance submission mechanism for excluded citizens. No Social Audit provision, MIS grievance portal, ombudsman, or Gram Sabha complaint process is mandated at scheme level for Vrikshopan Yojana. Since the scheme is open to all registered households (not an exclusive allocation to a limited group), the population of 'non-beneficiaries' is limited to non-registrants — but no mechanism exists for them to contest exclusion. FLAG: governance gap recorded.
Q24.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Vrikshopan Yojana Portal; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q25.r: The scheme's Economic Survey entry and Outcome Budget explicitly record employment generation as a scheme output: 'Nursery workers (12,500) + distribution support (8,500)' = 21,000 total workers. The Official Notification (2024 amendment) identifies implementation through Forest Department + Gram Panchayat + community participation, with nursery operations and distribution logistics creating wage employment. The scheme thus mandates an explicit livelihood pathway (wage employment in nursery production and sapling distribution) articulated in official scheme documents, satisfying condition (b) of T3.1 — an explicit livelihood pathway articulated in scheme design, even if expressed in terms of worker headcount rather than a formal log-frame income indicator.
Q25.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Outcome Budget 2025-26

observed.calc: No independent evaluation study, MIS income tracking, or household income survey for beneficiary households or nursery workers found in source documents. CAG audit verifies distribution and survival metrics only — no income per household data recorded.
observed.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

projected.calc: Scheme guidelines (Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN) and DPR-equivalent budget document do not contain a projected income per beneficiary household per year (ΔY). The Outcome Budget tracks environmental outputs (survival rate, hectares, CO2 potential) and scheme throughput (saplings distributed, households reached) but provides no income projection for the 185,000 beneficiary households. Employment headcount (21,000 workers) is recorded but wage rate and annual income per worker are not specified in any source document.
projected.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q26.r: Neither Observed nor Projected ΔY is available in scheme source documents. All source documents track environmental outputs (survival, green cover, CO2) and scheme throughput (saplings, households, species). No income-per-household data — observed or projected — is recorded for the 185,000 beneficiary families or the 21,000 employed workers. Q26 is UNVERIFIED and cannot be assessed against either threshold (₹3,000 observed or ₹5,000 projected).
docs: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — employment headcount only, no ΔY
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — environmental and throughput metrics only
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — distribution and survival verification only

Q27.r: One distinct livelihood pathway explicitly articulated in scheme documents: (1) Wage employment in nursery production and sapling distribution operations — Economic Survey records 12,500 nursery workers and 8,500 distribution support workers = 21,000 total; Outcome Budget confirms 'Workers: 21,000 (nursery + distribution).' This is a single pathway category (wage employment in scheme operations). Potential long-term income from fruit/timber/medicinal trees planted by the 185,000 beneficiary households is not articulated as a current income pathway in scheme guidelines or DPR — the scheme documents do not project or mandate a produce-income mechanism for planted trees. Score = 1.
Q27.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q28.r: No market or institutional offtake mechanism exists or is mandated for any produce or services generated through this scheme. The scheme distributes saplings for environmental objectives; it does not establish: MSP procurement for produce from planted trees, government agency offtake agreement (TDCCOL/OMC/NAFED) for fruit/timber/medicinal produce, MGNREGS wage payment linkage for nursery workers, GI tag or certification with documented buyer network, BMC access and benefit-sharing agreement, community seed distribution network, fair trade or organic certification with institutional buyer, or state procurement for community produce. Marketing of produce from privately planted trees is left entirely to individual households on open market with no institutional backstop. AUTOMATIC T3 FAIL on this gate.
Q28.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q29.r: Scheme MIS and evaluation framework track environmental metrics only: sapling survival rate (65% at 1-year), green cover (62,500 ha equivalent), CO2 sequestration potential (75 lakh tons, 30-year), species diversity (185 varieties), and nursery quality (92% compliance). No non-monetary livelihood indicators are tracked: food security months, nutritional diversity (food species accessed from trees), livestock feeding days, or access to medicinal plants for beneficiary households. Since Q26 ΔY is UNVERIFIED (cannot establish proximity to threshold), the near-threshold flag for potential under-measurement is noted as a general concern: the scheme's fruit, timber, and medicinal tree distribution likely produces non-monetary livelihood value that is entirely unmeasured.
Q29.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

pass_verification.enh_T3.5_flag: Non-monetary benefits from fruit, timber, and medicinal trees distributed to 185,000 households are entirely unmeasured; ΔY itself is unverified — true economic value is likely significantly under-measured by available scheme metrics.

t4a.data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; t1_output.json (carry_for_later_tests)

Q30.r: No legally binding or scheme-guideline-mandated replenishment/regeneration rule with a defined non-compliance consequence is found for the commons resource (government nurseries, classified A2). The scheme's Official Notification (2024) specifies: (a) '60%+ survival target at 1-year' — this is a performance metric for privately planted trees, not a replenishment obligation for the commons (nursery) resource; (b) '3-year maintenance support' — advisory/extension support for private plantations, not a commons replenishment mandate; (c) nursery restocking is implicit in the annual budget cycle (₹20 cr nursery establishment per FY), but this is operational continuity, not a scheme-guideline-mandated replenishment rule with a stated consequence (fund withholding, disqualification, reduced allocation) for non-compliance. No scheme clause mandating nursery restocking targets with enforcement consequences was identified. RESULT: T4.1 = No → AUTOMATIC T4 FAIL (Near-SOC Structural). The scheme generates sapling distribution from public nursery resources but contains no structural lock against nursery depletion or afforestation shortfall.
Q30.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q31.r: Carried T1.1 = A2 (state-managed government nurseries — a physical/natural commons category). T1.1 ≠ E, therefore the ρ Ratio track applies. Q32 is answered; Q33 is set to SKIP.

Q32.calc: ρ = Annual extraction from commons ÷ Annual replenishment mandated/created by scheme. Proxy method (T1.5 = No; resource un-demarcated; Estimated): Extraction = saplings distributed from nursery stock per year = 2.5 crore (FY 2025-26 target, confirmed by CAG and Outcome Budget). Replenishment = new sapling production capacity per year = 2.5 crore (annual nursery production target per scheme design, ₹20 cr nursery establishment budget supporting 85 government nurseries producing 2.5 cr saplings). ρ = 2.5 cr ÷ 2.5 cr = 1.00.
Q32.interp: PASS on ratio — ρ = 1.00 ≤ 1.2 threshold. However, this result is Estimated (T1.5 = No; no demarcated resource boundary; proxy method used: annual distribution = extraction, annual production target = replenishment). NOTE: Although ρ ≤ 1.2 (ratio PASS), T4a overall FAIL is driven by Q30 (T4.1 = No — no mandatory replenishment rule with enforcement). The ρ calculation also reflects a design feature: the scheme produces saplings for distribution, so extraction and production are equal by design — this does not represent a genuine sustainability lock. Uncertainty: T1.5 = No means this ratio cannot be verified against measured boundary data; re-verify when nursery demarcation data and audited production vs distribution records are available.
Q32.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

t4b.data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal; t4a_output.json (carry_for_t4b)

Q35.r: The scheme's primary commons resource (A2: government nurseries, 210 facilities state-wide) is not classified under a specific notified climate/ecological stress designation in the applicable official databases. The nursery infrastructure as a state-managed asset network does not appear on CGWB over-exploited block lists (groundwater), ISFR high-fire-risk or high-deforestation-pressure designations for the nurseries per se, or NDMA drought-prone block classification for the nursery resource specifically. While Chhattisgarh has some drought-prone districts and faces forest cover pressures (per ISFR), the scheme operates state-wide through 210 distributed nurseries and no specific stress-zone classification is documented for the scheme's commons resource in official sources. Q34 threshold remains at ≥ 1 (standard; no upgrade applied).
Q35.src: CGWB groundwater status reports; ISFR 2023; NDMA drought-prone district list; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q34.r: Annual monitoring is conducted by the Forest Department and verified by CAG. The scheme tracks: sapling survival rate (65% at 1-year, verified by CAG monitoring plot surveys), total saplings distributed (annual government reports), nursery production (210 nurseries audited), quality compliance (92% verified by sample testing). The Outcome Budget and CAG State Accounts Report produce annual performance data. The scheme portal (vrikshopan portal) provides aggregate statistics updated annually. However, monitoring is conducted by government agencies only (Forest Department + CAG audit); it is government-reported at annual intervals, not community-accessible at quarterly frequency or better. Score = 1. Q35 = No → threshold = ≥ 1. PASS on T4.3.
Q34.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal

Q36.r: No penalty or sanction mechanism for violation of replenishment or use rules is identified in any scheme source document. The Official Notification (2024) specifies distribution procedures and survival targets but contains no enforcement clause, fine, scheme-benefit withdrawal, suspension of nursery access, or legal sanction for non-compliance with any scheme obligation. The 60% survival target is a performance indicator with no stated consequence for failure to achieve it. There is no GP-imposed sanction or administrative penalty mechanism. Note: this finding is compounded by Q30 (T4.1 = No) — since no replenishment rule exists, there is correspondingly no penalty framework for its violation.
Q36.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

Q37.r: No post-scheme continuity mechanism is designed into the scheme. The scheme provides a 3-year maintenance support period for planted saplings, but this support period is within the scheme's active phase, not a post-scheme institutional handover arrangement. Once the scheme's support period ends, trees on private land revert to private management with no commons governance continuity. For the nursery infrastructure (A2 commons resource), no O&M fund, GP/BMC institutional vesting, or continuity clause for nursery operations after scheme closure is stipulated in scheme guidelines. The scheme assumes ongoing budget allocation from the state government, but no ring-fenced continuity fund or institutional vehicle beyond the scheme's project period is established. FAIL on T4.5.
Q37.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q38.r: Monitoring is conducted exclusively by government agencies (Forest Department annual monitoring, CAG periodic audit). No scheme guideline mandates community participation in monitoring: no social audit provision, no GP-led participatory resource assessment, no community monitoring committee with defined role, no Village Ecological Register update requirement, no beneficiary-led survival reporting beyond the portal's self-reporting feature (which is informational, not constitutionally or scheme-mandated). The portal's survival tracker allows individual beneficiaries to report tree survival but is not a structured community monitoring mechanism with institutional mandate or formal record-keeping role. FLAG: T4.6 = 0 is the primary structural gap and upgrade lever — community monitoring is the most cost-effective addition to convert Near-SOC (Structural) schemes. Activation plan recommended: mandate GP-level participatory sapling survival audits with recorded resolutions, feeding into the state MIS.
Q38.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25
